note: Authoritative local data from L1 chains.db (DeFiLlama + CoinGecko + growthepie/Dune). Raw signals, no scores. null = NO DATA (do not guess). FRESHNESS: price & market caps are live (today); TVL/stablecoin LEVELS are the latest available (carried forward if a chain's today-point isn't in yet); daily-FLOW metrics (DEX volume, fees, active_addresses, tx_count) are the LAST COMPLETE UTC DAY (today is partial and excluded) — full-day figures, use them as-is. SHARES are each chain's % of the WHOLE DeFi market (all ~500 chains; denominators in market.total_*), so tracked chains sum to <100% and deltas are immune to which chains we track. TVL/volume are USD-denominated, so share moves in a big market swing partly reflect token price, not pure flows. Each chain ALSO carries its OWN raw TVL growth — tvl_change_1d/7d/30d_pct (chain's level vs itself N days ago); use THESE for 'is this chain's TVL growing', NOT the share deltas (share = slice of market, a different question). If tvl_suspect_1d is true the 1d move is a likely upstream data glitch (a >25% one-day swing) — discount the 1d figure and lean on the 7d/30d trend instead.

emerging_candidates.method: longshot-style L1/L2 momentum + DefiLlama TVL growth, radar excluded
emerging_candidates.how_to_use: RAW watchlist of non-radar chains rising on the signals below — NOT buy calls. A candidate becomes a LEAD only on confluence (also trending / in a heating narrative / has a catalyst / fresh dev-gov) or a confirmed on-chain TVL surge.

signal_glossary.volume_heat: Volume is large vs market cap (turnover ≥15%) — unusual interest building.
signal_glossary.vol_leads_price: Volume is hot but 7d price hasn't run yet — interest building BEFORE the move (the early sweet spot).
signal_glossary.momentum: 7d gain keeping pace with / exceeding 30d — the move is accelerating, not fading.
signal_glossary.low_float: Only a small share of supply circulates — CAUTION: unlock overhang can cap upside.
signal_glossary.tvl_surge: On-chain TVL jumped ≥25% in 7d from a small base — real capital arriving, not just token price.

narrative.method: CoinGecko category momentum (24h mcap change + constituent 7d breadth/median); thematic narratives only (chain-ecosystem/stablecoin categories excluded)

events.subject: Plasma (XPL) US-purchaser unlock cliff
events.note: 2.5B XPL = 25% of total supply; zero-fee chain has no offsetting capture

catalysts.note: Curated dated catalysts (edit catalysts.json). DeFiLlama unlocks API is Pro-gated; the routine news-scan supplements. Fold these into the Open Items ledger.

attention.source: CoinGecko /search/trending (search/view velocity = retail attention proxy; LunarCrush free tier is locked/402)

pre_winner_lookalikes.method: validated winner-DNA coil screen (deep-below-high + oversold + at-30d-low [all validated, hold OOS] + below-ATH/high-vol/mid-float/small-cap); primed-but-not-yet-moved. NECESSARY-not-sufficient setup — needs an event trigger.
pre_winner_lookalikes.how_to_use: Beaten-down coils matching the data-validated winner DNA. SETUPS, not buys: most never move. Edge comes ONLY from confluence — a lookalike that is also trending / in a heating narrative / has a near catalyst / has fresh dev-gov.

signal_glossary.deep_below_60d_high: Trading <50% of its 60-day high — deeply beaten down. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample).
signal_glossary.oversold_30d: Down >25% over 30 days — oversold. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample).
signal_glossary.at_30d_low: Trading within ~3% of its 30-day low RIGHT NOW — the capitulation launch zone. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample); a bounce off the low actually LOWERS winner odds, so this is a timing edge.
signal_glossary.oversold_90d: Down >40% over 90 days — sustained downtrend (supporting context).
signal_glossary.deep_below_ath: Trading <15% of all-time high — far from prior peak, room to recover.
signal_glossary.high_vol: Elevated 30d volatility — capable of large moves.
signal_glossary.mid_float: Meaningful but not-fully-diluted float — the winner sweet spot.
signal_glossary.small_cap: Sub-$100M market cap — small enough to multiply.

dev_governance.note: Pre-announcement layer (early, low-crowd): GitHub releases/commit-activity + Snapshot governance for tracked protocols. A fresh_release or a pending 'launch/fund X' proposal precedes the marketing post. Each item carries release_notes / body (truncated) + a keyword dimension_hint (raw, NOT a verdict) — the FOLLOW-UP is to read these and judge what the change actually does and whether it improves the protocol at a fundamental level (consensus/scaling/security/economics/new capability) vs routine housekeeping. No composite score.

dev.release_notes: Hotfix release. Binaries are pre-built and attached as assets. Source for this release is not published in this repository.

## Protocol
#### Sui Protocol Version in this release: `130`

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27157: Protocol version 130. Unsettled object-funds withdraws are now recorded using per-account net amounts from transaction effects instead of running-max withdraw amounts, so withdraws that are offset by deposits in the same transaction no longer block other withdraws from the same account within the same consensus commit.

## Nodes (Validators and Full nodes)

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27203: Improved the error message returned when a transaction's address balance withdrawal exceeds the available balance: it no longer exposes internal accumulator object IDs, and now names the coin type and owner address and clarifies that Coin objects owned by the address are not part of its address balance.

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27149: Removes the `admission-queue-bypass-fraction` validator config field; all transactions are now admitted through the gas-price priority queue. The removed field is ignored if still present in a config file, but should be deleted.

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27062: Validators automatically recover from checkpoint and transaction forks on restart when the network has certified the canonical outcome and a new binary version is deployed; forks caused by the running binary version, or without a certified outcome, halt the node awaiting a corrected binary or operator-supplied fork recovery …[truncated]

## What's Changed
* Backport `release/v6.6`: fix(giga): route EVM validation failures to v2 fallback (CON-368) by @seidroid[bot] in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3757
* Backport `release/v6.6`: Update v6.6 changelog in prep to cut rc3 by @seidroid[bot] in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3746
* Bump version in prep to release v6.6-rc4 by @bdchatham in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3766
* Backport `release/v6.6`: fix(giga): fall back to v2 on execution errors by @seidroid[bot] in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3781
* Backport `release/v6.6`: Update v6.6 changelog in prep to cut rc4 by @seidroid[bot] in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3782


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/compare/v6.6.0-rc3...v6.6.0-rc5

This is a testnet release.

## What's Changed
* v4.2: Loads accounts in parallel for lt hash updates of stake accounts (backport of #13696) by @mergify[bot] in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13752
* v4.2: receive_and_buffer: remove expensive status-cache check (backport of #13437) by @mergify[bot] in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13703
* v4.2: xdp: improve packet drop perf (backport of #13658) by @mergify[bot] in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13723
* v4.2: status_cache: use SmallVec instead of Vec for tx fork status (backport of #13876) by @mergify[bot] in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13893
* v4.2: Handle dead keys secondary (backport of #13717) by @mergify[bot] in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13818


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/compare/v4.2.0-beta.1...v4.2.0-beta.2

## What's Changed
* miner: recover veblop producer stall when a build wedges with no pending task by @lucca30 in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2287


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/compare/v2.9.1-beta...v2.9.1-beta2

```
CODE_COLOR: CODE_YELLOW_MAINNET
RELEASE_VERSION: 2.13.1
PROTOCOL_UPGRADE: TRUE
DATABASE_UPGRADE: FALSE
SECURITY_UPGRADE: FALSE
```

This release includes a fix for the code that is a part of the protocol version 85.

Protocol upgrade voting time stays the same as [2.13.0](https://github.com/near/nearcore/releases/tag/2.13.0).

Voting for protocol version 86 will start on **Monday July 20th 00:00 UTC**.
**To continue participating in consensus, you need to upgrade your node before this time.**

If voting succeeds in one epoch, the protocol upgrade to version 86 is expected to happen 7-14 hours after the voting epoch ends.

![image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/berachain/beacon-kit/refs/heads/main/.github/assets/banner.png)

## Summary

This release accompanies the Osaka1 EL hard fork on bepolia. Osaka1 is EL-only, so **no beacond upgrade is required for the fork**. Activation details and node upgrade instructions are in the execution client release: [bera-reth v1.4.3-rc.0](https://github.com/berachain/bera-reth/releases/tag/v1.4.3-rc.0).

This release also updates the network eth-genesis.json files with the Osaka1 schedule, bepolia activates on July 22, 2026 16:00 UTC and mainnet keeps a placeholder time for now. These files live in the repository itself and are not attached as release assets.

## Update Priority

This table provides priorities for which classes of users should update particular components.

| User Class           | Priority        |
|----------------------|-----------------|
| Payload Builders     | Optional          |
| Non-Payload Builders | Optional          |

## All Changes

- feat: osaka1 fork to floor the base fee and blob fee (#3141)
- fix(ci): fix failing vulncheck via Go 1.26.5 bump and advisory suppression (#3144)
- fix(ci): pre-build the Go coverage tool to fix flaky coverage test failures (#3143)
- chore(deps): bump go-ethereum to v1.17.4 (#3140)
- ci(nightly): add missing pipeline permissions (#3139)
- ci(nightly): run full pipeline and deps tests nightly against main (#3137)
- fix(vulncheck): build govulncheck against patched …[truncated]

> [!NOTE]
> **Optional upgrade.** New extension points: `OpPoolBuilder` transaction ordering and validator are now injectable ([#21531](https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/pull/21531)), and `op-alloy-network` makes its reqwest dependency opt-in ([#21175](https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/pull/21175)). Also fixes Prometheus metric-bucket draining in the op-proofs CLI ([#21596](https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/pull/21596)). No consensus or operator-facing behavior changes.

## What's Changed in op-reth/v2.4.0
* feat(op-reth): make OpPoolBuilder ordering and validator injectable by @op-will in [#21531](https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/pull/21531)
* fix(op-reth): drain Prometheus metric buckets in op-proofs CLI by @dhyaniarun1993 in [#21596](https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/pull/21596)
* feat(op-alloy-network): make reqwest opt-in by @fakedev9999 in [#21175](https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/pull/21175)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/compare/op-reth/v2.3.3...op-reth/v2.4.0

🚢 Docker Image: https://us-docker.pkg.dev/oplabs-tools-artifacts/images/op-reth:v2.4.0
<!-- generated by git-cliff -->

This release is available as a Docker Image on Docker Hub at `offchainlabs/nitro-node:v3.11.2-3599aca`
This Docker image specifies default flags in its entrypoint which should be replicated if you're overriding the entrypoint: `/usr/local/bin/nitro --validation.wasm.allowed-wasm-module-roots /home/user/nitro-legacy/machines,/home/user/target/machines\`

If you're running a validator without a split validation server (this will be true of most validators), you should instead use the image offchainlabs/nitro-node:v3.11.2-3599aca-validator which has the extra script `/usr/local/bin/split-val-entry.sh` as the entrypoint.

## [v3.11.2](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/compare/v3.11.1...v3.11.2) - 2026-06-30

### Configuration

- Add `--node.dangerous.always-fallback-to-parent-chain-da` to support retiring chains off AnyTrust. [[commit]](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/commit/d71b69788edf8fd41385dcfadd9c8ee3c5ab1481)
- Add `--execution.transaction-filtering.address-filter.s3.preallocate-memory` (default true) to preallocate and commit the hash-list memory at startup, engaging only when `s3.max-file-size-mb` is set. [[commit]](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/commit/1ca3775819688f9211f96bb8a33ad5bcc10aae9c)

### Added

- Added consensus-v61 to dockerfile, remove rc and older consensus. [[commit]](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/commit/e53631372daa8627f8ef086324bb5e3457ee1b46)

### Changed

- Default `--file-logging.max-backups` increased from …[truncated]

## What Changed

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Base Mainnet**: Operators must upgrade to this release by June 25th at 18:00:00 UTC for the Beryl hardfork. See [here](https://docs.base.org/base-chain/specs/upgrades/beryl/overview) for more information.
> **Base Sepolia**: Recommended upgrade to improve Flashblocks stability

### base-reth-node
- Beryl Support for Mainnet
- Fix Flashblock processing where transactions relying on the BLOCKHASH could have different execution outcomes during Flashblocks and canonical processing.

### base-consensus
- Beryl Support for Mainnet

governance.title: [Constitutional] AIP: Ratification of Security Council Election Process Improvements
governance.body:
## Abstract

In September 2025, the ArbitrumDAO showed [varying degrees of support](https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/constitutional-aip-security-council-election-process-improvements/29848/54) for five Security Council Election process improvements via a [temperature check](https://snapshot.org/#/s:arbitrumfoundation.eth/proposal/0xb41324ddcc115d08149b192e0b8ae4cfad0ef39cecb5663ea63d8a460d2cb3cc). This updated temperature check aims to ratify the DAO’s support for the inclusion of four and omission of one of the originally proposed improvements, ahead of an on-chain vote.

The following improvements will be included:

1. Increase the Security Council cohort duration from 1 year to 2 years, therefore reducing election frequency from twice a year to once a year.

2. Reduce the qualification threshold in the Nominee Selection phase, from 0.2% to 0.1% of votable ARB.

3. Enable Security Council candidates to rotate their keys during the Compliance phase of the election.

4. Enable Security Council members to rotate their keys at any time during their term.

The following improvement will be omitted:

1. Allow existing Security Council members who re-apply to the Security Council to …[truncated]
